an5.78:1.1: “Monks, these five future dangers are just enough, when considered, for a monk—heedful, ardent, & resolute—to live for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized.
an5.78:1.3: “There is the case where a monk reminds himself of this:
an5.78:1.4: ‘At present I am young, black-haired, endowed with the blessings of youth in the first stage of life.
an5.78:1.5: The time will come, though, when this body is beset by old age.
an5.78:1.6: When one is overcome with old age & decay, it is not easy to pay attention to the Buddha’s teachings. It is not easy to reside in isolated forest or wilderness dwellings.
an5.78:1.7: Before this unwelcome, disagreeable, displeasing thing happens,
an5.78:1.8: let me first make an effort for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized, so that—endowed with that Dhamma—I will live in peace even when old.’
an5.78:1.9: “This is the first future danger that is just enough, when considered, for a monk—heedful, ardent, & resolute—to live for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized.
an5.78:2.1: “And further, the monk reminds himself of this:
an5.78:2.2: ‘At present I am free from illness & discomfort, endowed with good digestion: not too cold, not too hot, of medium strength & tolerance.
an5.78:2.3: The time will come, though, when this body is beset with illness.
an5.78:2.4: When one is overcome with illness, it is not easy to pay attention to the Buddha’s teachings. It is not easy to reside in isolated forest or wilderness dwellings.
an5.78:2.5: Before this unwelcome, disagreeable, displeasing thing happens,
an5.78:2.6: let me first make an effort for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized, so that—endowed with that Dhamma—I will live in peace even when ill.’
an5.78:2.7: “This is the second future danger that is just enough, when considered, for a monk—heedful, ardent, & resolute—to live for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized.
an5.78:3.1: “And further, the monk reminds himself of this:
an5.78:3.2: ‘At present food is plentiful, alms are easy to come by. It is easy to maintain oneself by gleanings & patronage.
an5.78:3.3: The time will come, though, when there is famine: Food is scarce, alms are hard to come by, and it is not easy to maintain oneself by gleanings & patronage.
an5.78:3.4: When there is famine, people will congregate where food is plentiful.
an5.78:3.5: There they will live packed & crowded together.
an5.78:3.6: When one is living packed & crowded together, it is not easy to pay attention to the Buddha’s teachings. It is not easy to reside in isolated forest or wilderness dwellings.
an5.78:3.7: Before this unwelcome, disagreeable, displeasing thing happens,
an5.78:3.8: let me first make an effort for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized, so that—endowed with that Dhamma—I will live in peace even when there is famine.’
an5.78:3.9: “This is the third future danger that is just enough, when considered, for a monk—heedful, ardent, & resolute—to live for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized.
an5.78:4.1: “And further, the monk reminds himself of this:
an5.78:4.2: ‘At present people are in harmony, on friendly terms, without quarreling, like milk mixed with water, viewing one another with eyes of affection.
an5.78:4.3: The time will come, though, when there is danger & an invasion of savage tribes. Taking power, they will surround the countryside.
an5.78:4.4: When there is danger, people will congregate where it is safe.
an5.78:4.5: There they will live packed & crowded together.
an5.78:4.6: When one is living packed & crowded together, it is not easy to pay attention to the Buddha’s teachings. It is not easy to reside in isolated forest or wilderness dwellings.
an5.78:4.7: Before this unwelcome, disagreeable, displeasing thing happens,
an5.78:4.8: let me first make an effort for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized, so that—endowed with that Dhamma—I will live in peace even when there is danger.’
an5.78:4.9: “This is the fourth future danger that is just enough, when considered, for a monk—heedful, ardent, & resolute—to live for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized.
an5.78:5.1: “And further, the monk reminds himself of this:
an5.78:5.2: ‘At present the Saṅgha—in harmony, on friendly terms, without quarreling—lives in comfort with a single recitation.
an5.78:5.3: The time will come, though, when the Saṅgha splits.
an5.78:5.4: When the Saṅgha is split, it is not easy to pay attention to the Buddha’s teachings. It is not easy to reside in isolated forest or wilderness dwellings.
an5.78:5.5: Before this unwelcome, disagreeable, displeasing thing happens,
an5.78:5.6: let me first make an effort for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized, so that—endowed with that Dhamma—I will live in peace even when the Saṅgha is split.’
an5.78:5.7: “This is the fifth future danger that is just enough, when considered, for a monk—heedful, ardent, & resolute—to live for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized.
an5.78:6.1: “These are the five future dangers that are just enough, when considered, for a monk—heedful, ardent, & resolute—to live for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized.”
